Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to act as an LLM analyzing event log data and 1 Identify anomalies and undesirable behaviors 2 Hypothesize why they occur 3 Propose SQL queries to investigate further

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me analyze the event log data against the assumed normal process flow Normal Process Flow 1 Register Order Sales 2 Perform Credit Check Finance 3 Validate Stock Warehouse 4 Confirm Shipment Logistics 5 Ship Goods Logistics 6 Issue Invoice Finance 7 Receive Payment Finance

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 1 Register Order SalesRep 01 2 Perform Credit Check CreditOfficer 1 3 Validate Stock WarehouseClerk 2 4 Confirm Shipment LogisticsMgr 1 5 Ship Goods WarehouseClerk 3 6 Issue Invoice FinanceTeam 01 7 Receive Payment FinanceTeam 02 Normal case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 1 Register Order SalesRep 02 2 Confirm Shipment LogisticsMgr 2 Should be credit check first and confirms shipment before even checking stock 3 Ship Goods WarehouseClerk 1 Shipping before credit check and stock validation 4 Perform Credit Check CreditOfficer 2 but way after shipping 5 Validate Stock WarehouseClerk 2 way out of order 6 Issue Invoice FinanceTeam 03 7 Receive Payment FinanceTeam 02 Major sequence violation Confirm Shipment and Ship Goods happened before Credit Check and Validate Stock Also Confirm Shipment and Ship Goods happened in the same minute register was at 08 20 confirm at 08 25 ship at 08 40 all happening very quickly before any upstream checks

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 1 Register Order SalesRep 03 2 Perform Credit Check CreditOfficer 1 3 Ship Goods WarehouseClerk 3 Should be Validate Stock Confirm Shipment first 4 Issue Invoice FinanceTeam 01 5 Confirm Shipment LogisticsMgr 2 After Ship Goods and has late confirmation Y 6 Receive Payment FinanceTeam 02 Ship Goods happened before Validate Stock and Confirm Shipment Confirm Shipment happened after Ship Goods late confirmation No Validate Stock at all

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 1 Register Order SalesRep 01 2 Receive Payment FinanceTeam 02 Should be last step 3 Issue Invoice FinanceTeam 01 Should be after shipment 4 Confirm Shipment LogisticsMgr 1 out of order 5 Ship Goods WarehouseClerk 4 out of order Even more extreme Receive Payment happened before almost everything else No Credit Check or Validate Stock at all Also Confirm Shipment has shipment scheduled N

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Anomalies 1 Sequence violations Activities happening out of the normal process order 2 Missing activities Some steps completely skipped eg Validate Stock missing in 1003 and 1004 Credit Check missing in 1004 3 Prematureshipment Goods shipped before checks completed cases 1002 1003 4 Late confirmation Confirm Shipment after Ship Goods case 1003 5 Premature payment Payment received before invoice and shipment case 1004 6 Negative Confirmations shipment scheduled N in case 1004 7 Performing activities before prerequisites Credit Check after Shipment Goods in case 1002

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypotheses System errors event logging out of order due to concurrency timing issues Policy violations skipping steps intentionally Training issues staff not following proper procedures Priority orders bypassing controls 1002 is priority Manual interventions or workarounds Race conditions in parallel department work

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now I need to craft SQL queries that could investigate these hypotheses Let me think about what queries would be useful 1 Find all cases where activities are out of order 2 Find cases with missing activities 3 Find cases where Ship Goods happened before Confirm Shipment 4 Find cases where payment was received before invoice 5 Check if resource roles match expected departments for activities 6 Look for patterns with priority orders 7 Find cases with negative indicators in additional info

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For out of order detection I need to assign expected positions Let me create a mapping Register Order 1 Perform Credit Check 2 Validate Stock 3 Confirm Shipment 4 Ship Goods 5 Issue Invoice 6 Receive Payment 7 Then I can compute whether events within a case follow the expected order

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Actually I need to think about NULL positions for missing activities and undefined activities The actual pos should be based on the event order not just defined activities Let me reconsider

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also I should check the role against expected department for each activity Register Order Sales Perform Credit Check Finance Validate Stock Logistics Confirm Shipment Logistics Ship Goods Logistics Issue Invoice Finance Receive Payment Finance

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me write the queries I will propose Ill present the anomalies first then hypotheses then SQL queries
